page: Google Cloud Platform c2d-standard-56 is a compute-optimized instance featuring 56 vCPUs and 224 GB of RAM. Powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor with an x86_64 architecture, this server utilizes 28 physical cores with 2.0 threads per core under a dedicated CPU allocation model. The processor includes 32 KB of L1d cache, 0.5 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. It supports hardware-level features such as Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX), AES and VAES cryptography, SHA_NI, and BMI2. This configuration does not include local NVMe storage or bundled GPUs, and provides zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark evaluations indicate strong multi-core processing capabilities, with stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core scores placing in the top 25%. Memory performance is highly polarized: the Passmark memory score ranks in the top 10%, and small-block cached read bandwidth is strong, whereas large-block uncached read and write bandwidths fall into the average tier. Cryptographic and mathematical execution is robust, as evidenced by top-25% scores in Passmark encryption and physics tests. For language model workloads, the instance achieves top-10% text generation speeds with small 135M models and top-25% speeds with medium 7B models, though prompt processing speeds remain average.

From a resource density perspective, the instance offers a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, delivering high compute density without the premium associated with memory-optimized hardware. The lack of local storage and GPU accelerators makes it a specialized choice for CPU-bound workloads that rely on network-attached storage. Based on its strong multi-threaded performance, software compilation with Clang, and database operation benchmarks, this server is suited for multi-threaded build pipelines, database hosting, Redis deployments, and medium-scale CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-standard-56 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server configured with 56 vCPUs and 224 GB of RAM. It is powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor featuring 28 physical cores with simultaneous multithreading and dedicated CPU allocation. The hardware lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. In benchmark testing, the instance demonstrates strong multi-core CPU performance, ranking in the top 25% for stress-ng and Geekbench multi-core tests, alongside a top-10% Passmark memory score. While uncached large-block memory bandwidth and prompt processing speeds are average, the server excels in multi-threaded tasks, cryptographic operations, and small-to-medium model text generation. This profile makes the instance highly efficient for CPU-intensive workloads such as software compilation, database operations, Redis deployments, and multi-threaded data processing.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-standard-56 is a compute-optimized server with 56 vCPUs, 224 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor, delivering strong multi-core performance and top-tier memory scores.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-standard-56 features 56 vCPUs, 224 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC CPU, offering strong multi-core and database performance.
tagline: A high-density, multi-core AMD EPYC processor instance configured with a balanced memory profile for intensive parallel computing and database workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor with 56 vCPUs and 28 physical cores
Equipped with 224 GB of RAM, featuring a top-tier Passmark memory score
Strong multi-core performance in software compilation and database operations
Optimized for CPU-bound workloads requiring dedicated resource allocation
Suited for Redis deployments, multi-threaded data processing, and CPU-based inference
